description: Optional account-sync service for signed-in users: gates every push on sign-in and the relevant opt-in, hard-gates health upload behind the health opt-in, reconciles prefs last-write-wins, and purges remote data on account deletion.
name: AccountSyncService

description: Pure deterministic training-history logic. groupByWeek rolls a newest-first workout list into per-week groups with weekly mileage, run count, and rest days. weeklyLoad classifies the current week via classifyTrend against a trailing multi-week baseline rather than a single prior week, so a steady progressive build reads as building not spiking, a true one-week jump reads spiking, a deliberate down week reads recovering with a mid-week guard, and too little history reads insufficient. loadHistory derives a compact newest-first weekly mileage series for the coach context. Monday-start POSIX calendar; drops unparseable dates.
name: ActivityHistory

description: The Today dashboard's entry point into Activity History: a full-width card button that opens the recent-workouts history overlay.
name: ActivityHistoryButton

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The day-one empty state for Activity History: a friendly Buddy prompt shown when no workouts have been logged yet.
name: ActivityHistoryEmptyState

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The Activity History title bar: Buddy avatar, screen name with a recent-workouts subtitle, and a Done button that dismisses back to the dashboard.
name: ActivityHistoryHeader

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The read-only Activity History screen: composes the header, per-week sections, and empty state over model.today.workouts, branching between the grouped history and the day-one empty prompt.

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

Satisfies App Store constraints (opaque, square, no text).

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the app-icon-showcase application scenario.

description: Production AppleCredentialChecking implementation: a thin passthrough that calls ASAuthorizationAppleIDProvider.getCredentialState(forUserID:) and returns the pure appleCredentialState mapping. Reports .unknown where AuthenticationServices is unavailable so a missing framework never drops the session.
name: AppleCredentialChecker

untestabilityReason.justification: Bare AuthenticationServices boundary call; the credential-state translation lives in the separately-tested appleCredentialState helper.

name: AskCoachEmptyState

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-empty-chat application scenario.

description: The Ask Coach title bar: a text-only screen name and an honest 'mock coach' subtitle naming Buddy, plus a trailing Weekly Review pill. No mascot here so Buddy appears once per screen, on each coach reply.
name: AskCoachHeader

description: The compose bar pinned to the bottom of the Ask Coach screen: a text field bound to the parent's draft and a send button that activates only when the draft has non-whitespace content.
name: AskCoachInputBar

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-empty-chat and ask-coach-run-or-rest application scenarios.

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-no-key application scenario.

description: The Ask Coach chat screen. Pure composition over AskCoachHeader, the ChatThread (or AskCoachEmptyState), and AskCoachInputBar. Owns the conversation state and the send/seed behavior: on send it appends the user's question and Buddy's CoachEngine reply; on appear it replays a seeded rbAskSeedQuestion so a scenario renders a real exchange.
name: AskCoachView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-run-or-rest and ask-coach-empty-chat application scenarios.

description: Three dots that pulse in sequence as a shared loading affordance.
name: BouncingDots

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the buddy-puffy-loader application scenario.

description: The Buddy mascot's mood with its presentation logic: a case-insensitive initializer from a raw coach string that falls back to ready, plus an accent color and a caption per mood used by the mascot halo and mood chip.

description: Hero card pairing Buddy and its mood chip with the step-goal ring.
name: BuddySummaryCard

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: A single chat row. User messages hug the trailing edge in a coral card; coach messages lead with a small Buddy and tint to the reply's mood, with an amber SAFETY FIRST shield when the answer carries a safety flag.
name: ChatBubble

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-run-or-rest and ask-coach-knee-pain application scenarios.

description: The scrolling conversation: a column of ChatBubbles that auto-scrolls to the newest message as the thread grows.
name: ChatThread

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the ask-coach-run-or-rest application scenario.

description: The AI coach recommendation card, with a calm brand treatment normally and an amber shield-marked treatment when the recommendation carries a safety flag.
name: CoachCard

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Buddy mock AI coach. Given a free-text question, the current TodayState, and the recent conversation history, it routes through CoachIntent and builds a curated, context-aware, safety-aware CoachReply. Pure and deterministic. Genuine safety signals still win: a genuinely hard run or a true load spike tilts recovery-ward with the safety flag, and sharp or worsening pain triggers the clinician-caution lockdown. But it trusts the athlete by default: ordinary post-run soreness gets calm reassurance without a flag, a routine easy run is no longer treated as hard, and a declared build is affirmed rather than rested. It threads conversation history to vary and de-repeat the closing check-in question, and abstains honestly when the load trend is insufficient.
name: CoachEngine

description: The intent classification behind the Ask Coach engine. Its classify(_:) does conservative keyword matching over the user's question into runOrRest, hit10K, mileageTooFast, injuryPain, postRunReflection, or a general fallback. Injury/pain is checked first so a safety-sensitive question is never mis-routed to upbeat coaching.
name: CoachIntent

description: Optional on-device personalization profile collected during onboarding: usual walking volume, usual walking time, other training, and a declared trainingPhase the coach respects as ground-truth intent. Every field is optional so a skipped step is nil or empty; trainingPhase makes an otherwise-empty profile non-empty and a legacy blob without it decodes as nil. Carried on TodayState into both coaches; leaves the device only on connected-coach requests, never analytics. isEmpty keeps an all-skipped profile off TodayState.
name: CoachProfile

description: UserDefaults-backed store for CoachProfile, following the RaceStore pattern (single JSON key, injectable defaults). load returns an empty profile when nothing is stored (so callers never handle a decode failure); save encodes the profile to JSON under one key; clear removes it. Written as the user advances through the onboarding personalization steps.
name: CoachProfileStore

description: A single prior conversation turn threaded back to the coach, carrying only role and text. Lets both the remote LLM coach and the offline CoachEngine build on the exchange instead of restarting each message, which is what stops the chat from repeating the same check-in question.
name: CoachTurn

description: The day-one empty state shown before Apple Health is connected: Buddy waving, a warm explainer, the connect call-to-action, and a privacy reassurance.
name: ConnectHero

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Root of the RunBuddy app. Reads the seeded TodayState at launch and shows either the day-one Connect hero or the full Today dashboard.
name: ContentView

description: The unit a custom race distance was entered in — miles or kilometers. The canonical stored value is always miles (RaceGoal.distanceMiles); DistanceUnit records the entered unit so display can honor it and RaceDistance.miles(from:unit:) can convert km to miles losslessly. String-raw-value Codable enum with a short label (mi/km).
name: DistanceUnit

description: A ViewModifier that applies the scenario-seeded Dynamic Type size to the whole app (via dynamicTypeSize) when rbContentSize is set; a no-op otherwise.
name: DynamicTypeOverride

untestabilityReason.justification: Thin SwiftUI environment adapter; its only logic is the dynamicTypeSize(forSeed:) mapping, which is unit-tested.

description: macOS-only dev tool (SwiftPM executable) that rasterizes AppIconArtwork via ImageRenderer and writes the opaque 1024x1024 AppIcon-1024.png, flattening onto the coral background so the output has no alpha channel. Regenerates the icon when the mascot art changes.
name: GenerateAppIcon

untestabilityReason.justification: A build-time CLI that renders SwiftUI to a PNG via AppKit/CoreGraphics and writes to disk; an OS/filesystem boundary tool, not shipping app logic.

description: SwiftUI Shape drawing an upward-curving arc used for Buddy's smiling eyes.
name: HappyArc

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI shape primitive; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the buddy-puffy-moods application scenario.

description: Non-shaming state shown when Health access is denied: explainer + Open Settings button. Reached only via the real HealthKit denial path in production.
name: HealthDeniedView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, and the denied state only arises from the real HealthKit flow.

description: Selects the activity data source at launch: SeededHealthDataSource when a CodeYam scenario seeded rb* keys, else the real HealthKitDataSource (production). isScenarioSeeded detects the seed.
name: HealthSource

description: The app's two top-level destinations behind the connected tab bar: today and coach. Its init(raw:) coerces a seeded rbStartTab string case-insensitively, falling back to today, so scenarios can land directly on the Ask Coach chat.
name: MainTab

description: Small pill under Buddy naming the current mood in its accent color.
name: MoodChip

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Pure launch-gating and step model for the personalized onboarding flow. hasSeen/markSeen/shouldShow decide whether to show the flow at launch (forced when rbStartScreen is onboarding, hidden once seen or under a scenario seed). introPageCount/personalizationStepCount/stepCount model the swipeable intro carousel plus the four personalization steps (goal, walking habits, other training, AI key); pageCount is a back-compat alias for the intro count. startPage reads and clamps rbOnboardingPage into 0..<stepCount so a scenario can target any intro page or personalization step for capture.
name: OnboardingState

description: Observable model for the Today dashboard. Reads the days TodayState from flat rb-prefixed UserDefaults keys seeded at launch, including a multi-week loadHistory series, and exposes derived values goalProgress clamped to 1.0, stepsRemaining, goalReached, plus a connect action. setTrainingPhase persists the declared training phase through CoachProfileStore and applies it to today.profile immediately so it reaches coaching, preserving the profiles other fields. ingestStravaWorkouts also populates loadHistory.
name: OtterpaceModel

description: Buddy the otter mascot in the puffy 3D style, drawn across all seven moods with small round ears, a broad soft muzzle, and glossy eyes.
name: PuffyBuddy

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the buddy-puffy-moods application scenario.

description: Scenario-only showcase rendering Buddy across every mood in a labelled grid.
name: PuffyBuddyGallery

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the buddy-puffy-moods application scenario.

description: Bouncy loading state for the puffy mascot with a squash-and-stretch hop, soft shadow, caption, and pulsing dots.
name: PuffyBuddyLoader

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the buddy-puffy-loader application scenario.

description: Registers/deregisters this device's APNs token against the persistent bearer session; a no-op without a session so server push stays strictly opt-in.
name: PushRegistrationService

description: Distance presets (5K, 10K, Half, Marathon, Custom) for the Settings race editor. Provides canonical miles and labels per preset, preset(forMiles:) to round a stored mileage back to a capsule within an epsilon (else Custom), clampMiles to bound/round to minMiles...maxMiles at one decimal, kmPerMile (1.609344), and miles(from:unit:) which converts a typed value in a DistanceUnit to clamped miles — miles rounded to one decimal, kilometers kept full-precision so the km value round-trips exactly.
name: RaceDistance

description: The add/edit race sheet presented from the Settings Races card: a name field, a distance capsule row of presets (5K/10K/Half/Marathon) plus a Custom option with a typed numeric field and a segmented mi/km unit toggle, a date picker, a location field, and optional notes. Custom distance is entered in the chosen unit and stored losslessly (km kept at full precision); the entered unit is persisted on the RaceGoal so editing reopens in that unit. Save builds a RaceGoal; Cancel dismisses.
name: RaceEditorView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the settings-races-list application scenario.

description: An optional upcoming race (name, distanceMiles, ISO date, location, notes, and the entered unit) carried as a list on TodayState so it flows into every coaching engine. distanceMiles stays the canonical value coaching keys off of; the optional unit records how the distance was entered (nil == miles). displayDistance formats both units honoring the entered one (a km race reads 15 km then 9.3 mi in parens; a miles race the reverse). Also carries the pure race helpers upcoming(in:asOf:), next(in:asOf:), hasUpcoming(in:asOf:), and daysUntil(date:asOf:) — used for build/taper/race-day coaching branches and the Today add-a-race banner gate, which shows whenever there is no upcoming race.
name: RaceGoal

description: The import-from-URL sheet opened from the Settings Races card. A URL field and Import button call the race-import backend with the user's BYO Anthropic key, then hand a prefilled RaceGoal seed plus the fields to double-check to the caller so the editor opens pre-filled. Mirrors AskCoachView's degradation: with no coach key it shows a connect-key explainer, and on any failure it surfaces a message plus an add-manually fallback, so the user is never blocked.
name: RaceImportSheet

description: A dismissable Today callout, in Buddy's voice, inviting the user to add a race. Shown only when the user has no races yet and hasn't dismissed it; tapping opens Settings (where the Races card lives) and the dismiss control hides it for good. Styled on the brand gradient card look so it reads as a native callout.
name: RacePromptBanner

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the today-race-prompt-banner application scenario.

description: A single UserDefaults-backed flag recording whether the user dismissed the Today add-a-race prompt banner, modeled on OnboardingState with an injectable UserDefaults for tests. isDismissed reads the flag; markDismissed sets it. No branching logic; it is a thin storage boundary over UserDefaults.
name: RacePromptState

untestabilityReason.justification: Thin UserDefaults read/write flag with no branching logic; the storage boundary is exercised via the Today banner scenario rather than a unit test.

description: The search-online sheet opened from the Settings Races card. A query field calls the race-search backend with the user's BYO Anthropic key and lists candidate races; tapping one hands its draft to the caller to open the editor pre-filled. Handles working, no-results, and error states, and degrades to manual entry when there is no coach key.
name: RaceSearchSheet

description: On-device persistence for the user's races: a single JSON-array UserDefaults key (otterpaceRaces) with an injectable UserDefaults, mirroring the workouts JSON-array pattern. Exposes load/save plus add/update/remove CRUD helpers that decode, mutate, and re-encode the array; update upserts by id and remove filters by id. Returns the mutated array so callers can update TodayState immediately.
name: RaceStore

description: HealthDataSource that serves the TodayState seeded by a scenario's rb* UserDefaults — the pre-HealthKit behavior, now behind the source seam so previews are unchanged.
name: SeededHealthDataSource

description: Pure policy: the server nudge is active (and the local nudge suppressed) only when signed in, health sync on, and a push token is registered.
name: ServerPushGate

description: Optional Sign in with Apple session state (undecided / guest / signedIn). Stores the Apple user identifier locally via an injectable TokenStoring (Keychain in prod). The session is durable: revalidate() confirms the stored Apple credential on launch/foreground (throttled per revalidationWindow), and only a genuine revocation ends it gracefully into guest via endSession() — transient/offline checks keep the user signed in. Never gates HealthKit.
name: SessionStore

description: The Settings overlay (reached via the gear on the dashboard and Connect hero): Account (sign out / delete account — the App Store-required deletion path), Apple Health status, daily step goal with quick presets plus a Custom stepper for any in-bounds value, a privacy explainer + policy link, and About/version.
name: SettingsView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, shown via the settings application scenario.

description: The optional welcome/sign-in screen: Buddy, a Sign in with Apple button, and an equal-weight 'Continue without an account'. Shown in production when undecided; previewable via the sign-in-welcome scenario.
name: SignInView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, shown via the sign-in-welcome application scenario.

description: A single labeled metric tile with an icon, value, and caption.
name: StatTile

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The three at-a-glance metric tiles: active minutes, distance, and time since moving.
name: StatsRow

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Circular progress ring toward the daily step goal with the count and a to-go or goal-hit caption.
name: StepRing

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Persists the two independent sync opt-ins (settings, and health off-by-default) plus the one-time health-consent acknowledgement; health sync cannot be enabled without consent.
name: SyncConsentStore

description: Pure last-write-wins reconciliation of a local value against an optional remote one, returning the winner plus whether to push local or adopt remote.

description: The flat design-token set for one app look — brand + semantic accents, ink/subtle text, card and background colors, and isDark. Five instances exist (Default, Bolt, Orbit, Fieldnote, Garden); the theme-resolved Palette reads the selected one so every screen retints.
name: Theme

description: The five selectable looks (default, bolt, orbit, fieldnote, garden). Each maps to its resolved Theme token set plus a display name and one-line education blurb shown in the onboarding step and the Settings picker. Default is the shipping identity and the default.
name: ThemeID

description: Owns the selected theme, persists it as the user's personal default in UserDefaults, and re-seeds from the rbTheme key for scenario previews (mirrors CoachProfileStore). A shared instance backs the theme-resolved Palette; screen roots observe it so switching re-themes the whole app live.
name: ThemeStore

description: The full Today surface composed from the header, Buddy summary, stats row, and the coach, latest-activity, and weekly-load cards. Exercised by the Today dashboard application scenarios.
name: TodayDashboard

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI composition view; this stack has no component-isolation harness, so it is demonstrated via the application scenarios on the simulator.

description: The dashboard's top header with the Today title, the day, and the RunBuddy wordmark.
name: TodayHeader

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Colored pill describing the weeks training-load trend, with distinct colors for spiking, recovering, building, and steady, and a neutral Baseline label for the insufficient case when there is not yet enough history to judge.
name: TrendBadge

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The app's text-role scale (largeTitle, title, body, caption, …) mapping each role to a SwiftUI text style with the brand rounded design and weights, so all text scales with Dynamic Type. Single source of truth replacing scattered fixed point sizes.
name: Typography

untestabilityReason.justification: A set of static Font constants (design tokens); no branching logic to test, exercised visually via scenarios.

description: Live URLSession-backed HTTP transport for the account/* sync endpoints; the network IO seam behind the injectable AccountSyncTransport protocol.
name: URLSessionAccountSyncTransport

untestabilityReason.justification: URLSession network boundary - the merge/gating logic it serves is unit-tested via the injectable transport seam in AccountSyncTests

description: Locally-persisted user preferences (no account): the daily step goal with a default, fixed preset options, and custom-goal bounds (minGoal/maxGoal/goalIncrement) plus clampGoal/isPreset helpers. Read by the production HealthKit path and edited in Settings (presets + Custom stepper).
name: UserPreferences

description: The week's training-load snapshot: mileage, run days, longest run, and rest days, with a trend badge.
name: WeeklyLoadCard

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Pure deterministic generator that turns the weeks WeeklyLoad context into a five-section weekly recap plus a Buddy mood and safety flag, classifying the week as empty, spiking, insufficient, sparse, or solid. The insufficient case recaps the week warmly but withholds a confident training-risk verdict, saying it is still gathering the baseline. Personalizes prose framing from the onboarding CoachProfile without altering classification, safety flag, or mood. Honors the conservative, never-shame-based, injury-aware rules.
name: WeeklyReviewEngine

description: The recap's closing one-focus-area callout — a single prominent next-action on the brand/gold gradient.
name: WeeklyReviewFocusCallout

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The Weekly Review title bar: Buddy avatar, screen name with a generated-recap subtitle, and a Done button that dismisses the recap.
name: WeeklyReviewHeader

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The recap hero: Buddy at the review mood, a mood chip, and the headline that sets the week's tone.
name: WeeklyReviewHero

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The training-risk section, taking the amber shield-marked safety treatment when the week's load warrants caution and a calm green treatment when risk is low.
name: WeeklyReviewRiskSection

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: One labeled prose section in the Weekly Review — a tinted icon, an uppercase title, and the recap copy on a standard card. Reused across the recap's standard sections.
name: WeeklyReviewSection

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: The read-only Weekly Review screen: composes the header, hero, the four prose sections, the training-risk section, and the focus-area callout over a WeeklyReview value, branching between the activity recap and the empty first-week prompt.
name: WeeklyReviewView

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Summary of the most recent run, walk, or ride: distance and pace headline plus duration, date, and source.
name: WorkoutCard

untestabilityReason.justification: Native SwiftUI view; no component-isolation harness on this stack, so it is shown via the application scenarios.

description: Register an APNs device token for a user, idempotent and preserving any existing movement/nudge state on the row.
name: addPushToken

description: Fixed-window in-memory rate-limit check: true when the key is under its limit for the current window. Best-effort, per-instance.
name: allow

description: Fetch a path from the live App Store Connect API with a bearer JWT (maintainer build-status script).
name: api

untestabilityReason.justification: calls the live App Store Connect REST API; standalone maintainer ops script with no offline harness, not part of the shipped app

description: Pure mapping from Apple's ASAuthorizationAppleIDProvider.CredentialState to the decoupled AppleCredentialState enum (authorized/revoked/notFound/unknown), with .transferred treated as inconclusive so the session is retained.
name: appleCredentialState

description: Gate the cron endpoint behind a CRON_SECRET bearer so it cannot be invoked publicly.
name: authorized

untestabilityReason.justification: reads process env and the request header at the Vercel cron auth boundary

description: Base64url-encode a buffer for the App Store Connect ES256 JWT (maintainer build-status script).
name: b64url

untestabilityReason.justification: pure base64url string transform with no branching logic, inside a standalone maintainer ops script

description: base64url-encode a Buffer or string with no padding — the JWT segment encoding used by the provider token.
name: b64url

description: Extract the raw bearer token from a request Authorization header, or null. Case-insensitive on the scheme.
name: bearerToken

description: Build the APNs alert payload (title/body + sound nested under aps).
name: buildAps

description: Resolve the expected Apple bundle id (the identity-token audience) from env, defaulting to the app bundle.
name: bundleId

untestabilityReason.justification: reads an env var with a constant default; no branching logic

description: Clamps a custom daily step goal into the minGoal...maxGoal range and rounds it to the nearest goalIncrement, so any user-dialed value is always in-bounds and on a clean 500-step rail.
name: clampGoal

description: Derive a client IP from the x-forwarded-for header for rate-limit keying.
name: clientIp

description: Mint a session for a verified user: generate a 256-bit token, store its hash in Supabase, and return the raw token (shown to the client once).
name: createSession

description: Deletes the user's account_health row entirely - the opt-out and delete-my-health-data path; tolerates a 404.
name: deleteHealth

description: Delete a user's entire push row — the sign-out / health-off / account-deletion opt-out.
name: deletePush

description: Revoke a session by token hash (sign-out / token rotation). Idempotent — a 404 is not an error.
name: deleteSession

description: Deletes a device's Strava token row from Supabase, tolerating a 404 and throwing on other errors.
name: deleteToken

description: Extract and validate the x-device-key request header, returning the trimmed key (empty string when absent/invalid).
name: deviceKeyFromHeader

description: Pure, case-insensitive mapping from a scenario-seeded rbContentSize string (e.g. xxxl, accessibility3) to a SwiftUI DynamicTypeSize; empty/unrecognized returns nil to honor the system size. Drives the app-wide Dynamic Type override for accessibility text-size scenarios.
name: dynamicTypeSize

description: Reads a required environment variable, throwing a typed missing_env error when it is unset.
name: env

description: Exchanges a Strava OAuth authorization code for tokens using the client secret, throwing on failure.
name: exchangeCode

description: Fetches recent Strava activities and maps them to the app's MappedWorkout shape, filtering out any that fail date validation.
name: fetchMappedActivities

description: Formats a whole number with locale thousands separators for readable step counts, e.g. 11240 becomes 11,240.
name: formatted

description: Returns a valid Strava access token for a device, refreshing against Strava and persisting the new token when the current one is near expiry.
name: freshAccessToken

description: Reads the signed-in user's account_health row from Supabase, or null when none exists.
name: getHealth

description: Reads the signed-in user's account_prefs row from Supabase, or null when none exists.
name: getPrefs

description: Read a user's account_push row (device tokens + mirrored movement + de-dup stamp), or null.
name: getPush

description: Reads a device's stored Strava token row from Supabase, returning null when none exists and throwing on a failed read.
name: getToken

description: Serverless endpoint for the optional health sync stream: GET reads the health row, PUT does a last-write-wins upsert, DELETE removes the row on opt-out.
name: handler

description: POST registers / DELETE deregisters this user APNs device token(s), bearer-authed via requireUser; the opt-in entry point for the server-driven nudge.
name: handler

description: Vercel endpoint /api/account/session: POST verifies an Apple identity token and mints a bearer session; DELETE revokes the current session (idempotent sign-out).
name: handler

description: Serverless endpoint for the settings/preferences sync stream: GET reads the prefs row, PUT does a last-write-wins upsert and rejects any payload carrying health fields.
name: handler

description: Stateless BYO-key coach API proxy. Accepts a question, the TodayState context, and optional conversation history, validates and bounds every input, then calls Claude with the curated injury-aware system prompt and returns a structured CoachReply. It rebuilds the history into a real multi-turn conversation so the coach stops repeating itself, and the prompt trusts the athlete by default while keeping genuine injury and load-spike caution. The key is used per request and never stored or logged.
name: handler

description: Scheduled scan applying the tested shouldNudge policy to each opted-in user and delivering the APNs nudge, stamping de-dup and pruning dead tokens.
name: handler

description: POST endpoint that imports a race from a URL. Validates the URL and blocks private/loopback/metadata hosts to prevent SSRF, fetches the page server-side with size and timeout caps and no redirect-following, then calls Claude with the caller's BYO Anthropic key to extract a structured race plus confidence and missingFields hints. Maps upstream 401/429 to invalid_key/rate_limited and degrades to an empty race on unparseable model output.
name: handler

description: POST endpoint that searches races by name. Calls Claude with the caller's BYO Anthropic key to propose up to five candidate races (name, optional date, distance, location, best-effort sourceUrl) for the user to pick from, capped and rate-limited. Degrades to an empty list on unparseable model output and maps upstream 401/429 like the coach proxy.
name: handler

description: GET endpoint that reads a device's Strava token, refreshes it when expired, and returns recent activities mapped to the app's workout shape (not-connected with an empty list when no token).
name: handler

description: OAuth redirect endpoint that bounces the browser back into the otterpace:// app scheme with the code (or error) plus state so ASWebAuthenticationSession can capture it.
name: handler

description: POST endpoint that forgets a device's Strava tokens via a server-side Supabase delete.
name: handler

description: POST endpoint that exchanges a Strava OAuth code for tokens and stores them in Supabase, returning only a success flag and the athlete first name.
name: handler

description: SHA-256 hex of a session token — only this hash is ever persisted, never the raw token.
name: hashToken

description: Builds the Supabase PostgREST URL for the account_health table from SUPABASE_URL.
name: healthEndpoint

description: Reduces fetched HTML to bounded readable text for extraction: strips script and style blocks and all tags, decodes a few common entities, and collapses whitespace, so the model sees the page's text without markup or token bloat.
name: htmlToText

description: Last-write-wins decision shared by both sync streams: true when there is no stored row or the incoming updated_at is strictly newer than the stored one.
name: incomingWins

description: Reports whether a step goal equals one of the fixed quick-preset options, used by Settings to decide between highlighting a preset capsule and the Custom chip.
name: isPreset

description: True when an hour falls inside the quiet window, correctly handling a window that wraps past midnight; start==end means never quiet.
name: isQuietHour

description: SSRF guard for the race-import URL. Accepts only http/https and rejects hosts pointing at the local network: localhost, .local, loopback, and private/link-local IPv4 ranges including the cloud metadata address 169.254.169.254. Returns the parsed URL when safe, or null to reject.
name: isSafePublicUrl

description: True when a device-key string matches the allowed 16–128 char [A-Za-z0-9_-] pattern.
name: isValidDeviceKey

description: Mint a short-lived ES256 App Store Connect API JWT from the local AuthKey (maintainer build-status script).
name: jwt

untestabilityReason.justification: signs a JWT with a local App Store Connect private key file for the live ASC API; standalone maintainer ops script, not part of the shipped app

description: Every push row with at least one token and a known last-movement time — the cron's scan set.
name: listNudgeCandidates

description: Maps a single Strava activity to a MappedWorkout, returning null when the date is missing or malformed; computes miles and pace.
name: mapActivity

description: Mirror the latest movement heartbeat (last-movement time + inactivity setting) onto an existing push row so the cron scans one table; no-ops when the user has no push registration.
name: mirrorMovement

description: Renders minutes-since-last-movement as a compact label: now at or below zero, bare minutes under an hour, and hours-plus-minutes above, dropping the minutes part when it is zero.
name: movementLabel

description: Validates and bounds the optional conversation history in the coach API. Keeps only well-formed user or coach turns, maps coach to assistant, drops malformed or oversized entries, and caps to the most recent turns so the multi-turn prompt stays small and the user's token spend stays bounded.
name: normalizeHistory

description: Formats a speed in meters per second as a mm:ss/mi pace string with zero-padded seconds.
name: pacePerMile

description: Defense-in-depth guard: true when a settings/preferences payload carries any health field, so the prefs endpoint can reject it and never leak health data into the wrong row.
name: prefsContainHealthFields

description: Builds the Supabase PostgREST URL for the account_prefs table from SUPABASE_URL.
name: prefsEndpoint

description: Converts an ISO yyyy-MM-dd date into a short weekday-and-day label such as Mon, Jun 22, returning the input unchanged when it is not a valid ISO date.
name: prettyDate

description: Build and ES256-sign the APNs provider JWT from the key id, team id, and .p8 signing key.
name: providerToken

description: Build the account_push Supabase table endpoint from SUPABASE_URL.
name: pushEndpoint

description: Remove one device token; drops the whole row when it was the last token so a signed-out user leaves no push footprint.
name: removePushToken

description: Authenticate a request from its bearer session token, returning the verified user id or null (handlers map null to 401). User id derives only from the token, never the request body.
name: requireUser

description: Rounds a number to one decimal place.
name: round1

description: Recursively walk a prefs payload (bounded depth) detecting any health-related key, backing prefsContainHealthFields so the prefs endpoint never stores health data.
name: scanForHealthKey

description: Deliver one alert to a device token over HTTP/2, best-effort, surfacing a prunable reason on an unregistered token.
name: sendPush

untestabilityReason.justification: APNs HTTP/2 network delivery, verified against Apple's sandbox not in CI

description: Supabase REST URL for the account_sessions table, derived from SUPABASE_URL.
name: sessionsEndpoint

description: Pure policy deciding whether to send a movement nudge to one user now: idle past their threshold, once per idle window (de-dup on last-nudge vs last-movement), and never during quiet hours.
name: shouldNudge

description: Stamp the last-nudge time after a successful send, for one-per-idle-window de-dup.
name: stampNudgeSent

description: Builds the VoiceOver spoken summary for the step-goal ring, mirroring the three caption states with whole-number step, goal, and remaining counts; never shame-based.
name: stepGoalAccessibilityValue

description: Builds the caption shown under the step-goal ring count: a celebratory crushed-goal line once steps pass the goal, a goal-hit line at exactly the goal, and otherwise the of-goal target framing.
name: stepGoalCaption

description: Clamps a goal-progress fraction to the drawable step-ring arc range; floors near zero so the rounded cap shows and caps a met or crushed goal at a full turn. Drives both the trim length and the gradient span.
name: stepRingFill

description: Builds the apikey + bearer Authorization headers for Supabase PostgREST from the service-role key.
name: supabaseHeaders

description: Builds the Supabase PostgREST URL for the strava_tokens table from SUPABASE_URL.
name: tokensEndpoint

description: Upserts the user's health/activity snapshot row into account_health via merge-duplicates.
name: upsertHealth

description: Upserts the user's settings/preferences row into account_prefs via merge-duplicates.
name: upsertPrefs

description: Merge-upsert a user's account_push row (tokens + mirrored movement + de-dup stamp) into Supabase.
name: upsertPush

description: Upserts a device's Strava token row into Supabase using merge-duplicates, throwing on a non-OK response.
name: upsertToken

description: Resolve the user id for a bearer token by its stored hash, or null when it is not a live session.
name: userIdForToken

description: Verify a Sign in with Apple identity token (issuer, audience, signature) against Apple live JWKS and return the stable user sub.
name: verifyAppleIdentityToken

untestabilityReason.justification: verifies tokens against Apple live JWKS endpoint (appleid.apple.com) with no deterministic offline path. The handler that consumes it is covered in test/api/session.test.ts with the verifier mocked.
